commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a per-UI-state recent chat-frame identity cache keyed by session, agent, run, sequence, and state, with controller tests for duplicate delta and final frames.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +65. Total +115 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main accepts every chat payload passed to handleChatEvent, and the PR body includes two redacted real-session observations where one durable assistant row produced duplicate WebChat bubbles; I did not run a browser repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer after-fix WebChat proof, not a narrow code defect that a repair lane can safely provide.

target.title: fix: WebChat duplicates external reply events
target.body:
Related: #85771

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users viewing WebChat could see the same assistant reply rendered twice when an external or plugin-returned reply arrived through duplicate live chat event delivery.

The observed failure was frontend-only: the affected WeChat/AKK session transcript contained a single assistant message, but WebChat rendered two identical assistant bubbles for the same reply.

## Why This Change Was Made

WebChat now treats gateway chat frames as idempotent by tracking recently accepted frame identities per UI state. The dedupe key uses the gateway event identity (`sessionKey`, `agentId`, `runId`, `seq`, and terminal/delta state), not message text, so legitimate repeated assistant text from later turns still renders normally.

This keeps the fix at the Control UI event-consumption boundary and avoids changing transcript persistence, plugin behavior, or gateway delivery semantics.

## User Impact

WebChat no longer displays duplicate assistant bubbles when the same live chat event reaches the client more than once. Normal repeated replies remain visible when they come from distinct turns or distinct event frames.

## Evidence

Real affected-session evidence, redacted:

```json
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq806y9qtmkjteaum8nyfvjtl8@im.wechat",
  "sessionId": "24b33b5d-4e7a-4fde-9e81-15bc1d6f3306",
  "provider": "deepseek",
  "model": "deepseek-v4-flash",
  "transcriptLines": 943,
  "messageRows": 904,
  "assistantRows": 442,
  "adjacentIdenticalAssistantRows": 0,
  "tailWindowMessagesChecked": 90,
  "tailAdjacentIdenticalAssistantRows": 0
}
```

This was checked from the local session transcript using role, text length, and SHA-256 text hashes only. No message content was printed or copied into the PR. The same WeChat/AKK session showed duplicated assistant bubbles in WebChat, while the durable transcript had no adjacent identical assistant rows, which points the fix at live WebChat event consumption rather than transcript persistence.

Second live repro, redacted:

```json
{
  "observedDuplicateText": "搞定了！DeepSeek API 配置成功，并且验证跑通 ...",
  "sessionId": "24b33b5d-4e7a-4fde-9e81-15bc1d6f3306",
  "transcriptLine": 1037,
  "messageId": "401ad5ce",
  "assistantTextSha256Prefix": "271f8e2cafa95fab",
  "targetTextTranscriptMatches": 1,
  "adjacentIdenticalAssistantRows": 0,
  "totalMessageRows": 995,
  "totalAssistantRows": 486,
  "fileLogExactTextHits": 0,
  "fileLogMessageIdHits": 0,
  "targetWindow": "2026-06-23T17:22:30Z..17:24:30Z",
  "targetWindowWeixinRows": 21
}
```

The target-window file logs show the WeChat channel polling through `getUpdates` fetch failures/backoff around the same time and a single prompt-build event for the same session. They do not contain a second copy of the target assistant text or message id. That gives a second real reproduction where the duplicated UI text maps back to one durable assistant row.

Focused regression coverage:

```text
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       131 passed (131)
```

Patch sanity:

```text
git diff --check -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
```

Local structured review:

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.84)
```

Manual source/log evidence:

- Gateway `broadcastChatFinal` emits ordered chat frames with `runId` and `seq`; WebChat previously passed every received `chat` event directly into `handleChatEvent` without a frame-level idempotency guard.
